headline: Why AI layoffs aren't giving stocks the boost companies wanted
summary: AI-driven layoffs have become a defining theme across the tech industry over the past year, with some sources estimating more than 112,000 U.S. jobs being lost to AI since the start of 2025. While major companies like HP and Amazon have often framed these cuts as a way to boost efficiency and shift focus toward new technologies, investors themselves don't appear convinced that these layoffs will improve companies' bottom lines in the long run.
